column4:
Let's have a conversation about how often the tournaments update the player's rank
2 things:
1. It feels bad and confusing to earn points and then see your rank go down.
2. In competitive tournaments, it's annoying to open and reopen that UI constantly to see if your spot changed. Could notifications help?

Could we update the rank while a player is in a shutdown or bankheist, before they go back to the roll screen and then update it when you get more points. (this is to avoid seeing your rank go DOWN when you earn points.) Please reduce cost of the refreshing and if possible, increase the frequency of the refreshing.
Ability to initiate autoroll during the jail sequence AND coin flip. Roll button should look like it does if you were already on auto-roll when you entered it

DOOBER OVERHAUL. There are many ways to improve the doobering system and we have several product tasks that all fall into this overhaul. We want to:
Speed up doobers 
Make them more satisfying to see/hear/feel
Make it clearer where they are going 
Match our comps in terms of quality 
Use less MRG and use more direct doobering when possible
Much more! We'll link to a full design in the solutions column
column17: Along with tracking all major KPIs pre/post launch, we want to ask CSAT questions via surveys to see if players feel like the game is going at their pace. And whether they agree that the game's reward moments reflect the rewards given.

column4:
1. Many players regularly heist inactive players, making the game feel dead.
This happens because heists are “pending” until the player logs back in. And a churned player isn’t going to log in, so the game still thinks they have enough money to be heisted.
The fix: Players who have at least {10} pending heists are ineligible to be heisted again.
Size: XS

Rolling doubles in jail doesn't contribute to the roll doubles in quick win and that feels lame
https://scopely.slack.com/archives/C02JC5M2K17/p1717432185396549

2. Having more friends massively increases the number of times you get heisted per day, disincentivizing having friends.
The fix: There’s currently a heist cooldown system, where Player A cannot heist Player B for X hours after heisting them. So, just make X bigger when Player B has more friends.
That way, if player B has few friends Player A can heist him again after 2 or 3 hours. But if player B has a ton of friends Player A can’t heist him again for another day or two.
Scales nicely with friend count!
Tap on a building to zoom in on it, tap again to zoom back out (or if you build a landmark it'll go over to that landmark as it normally does). Including this functionality on board complete.

Improve pill intro cascade: Yes the pills intro one by one from top to bottom (cascade). I think this task was to accelerate the cascade so the interval between pills appearing gets shorter going down the stack. So the overall the time is reduced and it looks better and is more consistent with other areas of the game. Check this is done in all scenarios (including after railroad game or coming back from other game)
CHEATING FIX. Fix multiplier results in plinko. If I roll on x100 and it goes down to 1, I roll again, I go back to x100 and I get a different results. Too easy to cheat.

4. There are protections to prevent Player A from heisting Player B too often, but they break if Player A is performing >10 heists a day.
This is because Player A’s heist cooldown list is only 10 entires long. So if you heist 11 people, it erases the first record and you can heist whoever used to be there even if the cooldown isn’t
The fix: Just make the heist cooldown list longer. 20 people? 30?
Size: XS

column4: Invest in improving the roll experience. Right now, sometimes you roll the dice and your token moves quickly, the dice move out of frame so you can't actually see what you rolled. To fix this, we can update the code to try and point the dice at a specific position that's in the view frame, within the bounds of the board, and somewhat ahead of the anticipated roll. This would make it easier for users to catch their roll numbers. Talk to Carl L.

Seasonal core board reward improvements - 

1. Add “BOARD REWARD” logo onto the board reward icon.
2. Remove tray behind landmark deeds, put board reward in pill & have the timer float in the top center. Have the pill’s background color be based on the reward.

column4: Some boards are significantly more interesting than others. We have ~370 boards, but for anyone who is past that content and seeing re-used boards (which many valuable players do), there is not great logic for which ones they see. Right now it is a simple weight based on how many times you see them. We should perhaps go through and rank them, to add an additional weight to their frequency (i.e. Noir board maybe shows up 3x more frequently than some basic one).... there is no RIGHT and WRONG board -> this is a matter of preference... BUT.... surely we can apply some rankings

In the first login of the day, when the system shows the results of the tournament, the screen is load by parts, first the frame and after the content without animations just a poping spawn. We can introduce the contentwith an animation or polish it to improve it quality

Time-to-roll

For Chain Offers, add the option to tap to skip the unlocking animation when claiming the reward. Currently, this creates significant friction and can feel frustrating when players want to quickly collect their rewards but are slowed down by the animation. This interruption takes players out of the flow and delays them from getting back to rolling.

Cosmetics

It’s been discussed many times that cosmetics effectively map the player’s journey, as they represent the main beats in the rollercoaster of progression. I’d love if we could explore further how each cosmetic could feel like a proverbial “stamp” on a player’s MGO passport.

As a starting point, I’d consider adding at least the date when the cosmetic was claimed, so players can look back and remember when they earned it. This would help reinforce the sense of progression and make cosmetics feel more like milestones tied to their personal history in the game.

Profile Pictures

This is one of the most player-facing elements in the game and the simplest way the player can customize his 'avatar' - yet the available options are rarely refreshed. The last update was in December ’24 for Christmas, and since then, we haven’t leveraged opportunities around IP or seasonal moments.

I’d also consider going a step further and introducing animated avatars as obtainable rewards. This could effectively create a new type of cosmetic commodity which, unlike most others (with the exception of shields), would be highly visible to other players and therefore has the potential to become a desirable status symbol in the game.

Explore more options to show players what vanity is at the end of Leagues. Right now it's impossible to see what vanity you can earn unless you get to the class before it OR you search for it on social media if someone has posted about it. Can we include a short section in the tooltip for future classes where we say something along the lines of "and in future classes ..." and then include the vanity there for players to tap and look at.

https://scopely.slack.com/archives/C02JC5M2K17/p1772639159459439
Interesting feedback, I think we'd honestly prefer players to use their profile pics from facebook here to make the game feel more alive. So it's kinda of a push and pull here of making these avatar images better could hurt the social feeling of the game?

column4:
Ruby Urlings  [5:03 PM]
Been thinking about bankheist and shutdown. I like the anger and deceit I feel coming back to the game, and seeing that my friend(s) destroyed my landmarks, especially since it’s so visual. It triggers a (funny) emotional reaction from me. However, I do not get that after someone bankrupted me. I think it’s partially as when I enter I can see it on the timeline, and I quickly dismiss it just like other pop ups. There’s no cool visuals making it obvious my friends stole from me, even though I know they did, and hence I have no hard feelings at all. Maybe this is an opportunity to show an animation or effect on my main hud with 0 balance, showing that e.g. Kurt robbed my cash. Something more visual and cheeky that equals seeing your landmark destroyed, to trigger more of an emotional response, rather than just a timeline entry. Just an idea.

They claim dice to be random. Yes. But everything else is rigged.

Introduce tap-to-skip animations throughout the game, prioritizing mini-games, which are currently the biggest contributor to delaying the players from rolling.

Long animations are increasingly becoming a source of friction throughout the game. We don't have the natural “tap to skip” functionality, which breaks the flow and can create strong moments of frustration.

The UI setup for the railroad reward popups (SD and BH) has limited scaling options which makes it look very odd on wide devices. Needs alignment between product/design and UI for how this should appear on wider screens and consideration for future requirements (e.g. assymetric theming - where the left side might look different to the right).
